description: purify1.co.za on winsvrweb09 (Windows IIS 10 / PleskWin). WordPress block editor cannot save pages — 403 on POST to /wp-json/wp/v2/pages/4?_locale=user. Client has valid auth, nonce, same-origin. Issues: (1) %2C in query string blocked — 1-grid Agent disabled ModSecurity rule 942200. (2) X-HTTP-Method-Override: PUT blocked — 1-grid Agent disabled ModSecurity rule 921110. (3) WAF option missing in plan — Jessica enabled it. (4) STILL 403 on POST to wp-json — persists after all fixes. Likely WAF now active (Detect mode) or IIS Request Filtering blocking POST/override on wp-json path.
outcome: In Progress - 403 persists. Need to check WAF logs on winsvrweb09 and verify ModSecurity exceptions applied on Windows IIS/Plesk (not on Linux Apache).
resolution_steps: Check Plesk WAF logs on winsvrweb09 for exact rule blocking POST to wp-json
Verify ModSecurity exceptions (942200, 921110) applied on winsvrweb09, not just on Linux
If WAF is in Detection Only mode, it should not block — check if switched to On
Check IIS Request Filtering → URL scan for verb blocking on /wp-json/
Disable WP Cerber (plugin) REST API restrictions if installed
symptoms: Only affects REST API POST, GET works fine

description: Cannot add as addon domain in cPanel - DNS entry already exists in cluster. Same as ubuntutrading.co.za. Fix: run killdns - /usr/local/cpanel/bin/whmapi1 killdns domain=danceboutiquedirect.co.za

info@pwflooring.co.za compromised - spammer authenticated via SMTP from IP 196.208.36.80 (Vox/DDII). Sent spam impersonating MWEB with fake invoice attachments. Valid DKIM. Customer reset password. Advised check forwarders, access logs, enable DMARC, scan device. Client confirmed: password reset done, no suspicious forwarders or access logs found. Awaiting confirmation if 196.208.36.80 is a known office IP.
Email delays reported. Missing PTR, MailChannels not in SPF. Previous ticket attributed to DDoS attack. Requested client send full email headers for analysis.
Emails landing in Gmail spam. SPF missing mail server IP 41.185.110.23. DMARC p=quarantine causes spam when SPF fails. Advised add ip4:41.185.110.23 to SPF.
SARS mail server blocking .wmf attachments from faeda@platinuminc.co.za to rmagodi@sars.gov.za. Recipient-side policy. Advised resend as PDF/ZIP or contact SARS IT.

Phishing page targeting 1-grid customers. Spoofs document viewer with Excel icon, loads fake 1-grid login via iframe on bim-r.ma:8443/gygy. Registrant: Ismail Khoubbaz (bimrengineering@gmail.com). Registrar: ADK MEDIA (Casablanca).
Client sent composed headers instead of delivery headers. Requested full raw headers from received email.
notes: Client needs to send headers from the received email (Show Original/View Source) including all Received: lines with timestamps.

description: Server hanging - customer could not RDP or ping. Rebooted via ILOM KVM console. Second occurrence in 24hrs - client called again and requested reboot. Both resolved.
Mac Mail showing SSL warning for mail.bellagio.co.za - self-signed cert cached. Installed Let's Encrypt cert in cPanel covering *.bellagio.co.za. Verified active on IMAPS:993 and SMTPS:465.
Client could not add domain online. Payment pending debit order 01.06.2026. Danielle activated domain. DNS records not yet configured.
Client not receiving emails from rgwsolutions.co.za (external domain hosted on Pressable/Automattic, IPs 199.16.173.152/199.16.172.95). Needs whitelisting check in SpamTitan and/or SpamAssassin.

FTP port 21 blocked on srv144 due to security breach. Client cannot login via FTP. Confirmed ports 21,22,990,2222 all closed. cPanel (2083/2096) working. Client rejects File Manager — needs FTP restored.
notes: All FTP/SSH ports blocked on srv144. Only cPanel accessible. Client still reporting not working after 9 days. Reseller (Globuz) manages this site.
resolution: Customer advised to use File Manager or wait for port restoration
Left for later follow-up — customer still not satisfied with File Manager workaround

description: Client reported SSL issue with mail.eduvisa.co.uk. Checked - current Let's Encrypt cert valid until 20 Jul 2026, covers mail.eduvisa.co.uk, properly served on IMAPS:993, POP3S:995, SMTPS:465. No renewal needed. Advised client to restart email client to clear cached cert.

Client could not install/remove WordPress via Softaculous on srv158. Connection lost error on both operations. Likely transient server resource pressure (59% swap, mariadbd 1.7GB). Resolved on retry — WordPress installed successfully at http://tmang.co.za/wp.
Same issue as tmang.co.za — connection lost on install/remove. Retried after tmang.co.za success and WordPress installed at http://south-street.co.za/wp. Resolved.

FTP port 21 blocked on srv144 due to security breach. Worked with client to upload/restore .wpress file (~1.6GB) via File Manager and All-in-One WP Migration. Updated .htaccess limits (upload_max_filesize 64M to 2048M). Provided WordFence + All-in-One WP Migration guidance.
SMTP outgoing email was suspended. Ran whmapi1 unsuspend_outgoing_email for the account per client request.
Client experiencing password prompt loop. 1-grid MX records but domain points to Google Cloud (34.111.179.208). In progress - awaiting server-side investigation.
Can send emails but cannot receive from external senders. Server: lnxsvrweb09. In progress - awaiting investigation.
500 internal server error. Custom nameservers configured. Server: srv11. In progress.
Microsoft blocklist S3150 on srv11 (41.185.8.111). Delisting via sender.office.com / olcsupport.office.com. In progress.
Disk usage at 97%. Need to notify Stefan Alberts. SOP: Small/Medium = upgrade; Large = buy another server; if declined = remove PRTG sensors. Notification drafted but not yet sent.
Intermittent email delivery failures to Standard Bank. Server: srv64. In progress.
Awaiting SpamTitan whitelist details. Domain hosted on Shopify externally, MX points to 1-grid. In progress.
Domain on srv11. CIRCL abuse report filed. Follow up pending.
Checked per request. External domain (host-h.net), not a 1-grid customer. No action taken.
Client cannot change nameservers to NS1/NS2.BLUEHOST.COM. Getting unexpected error. Root cause: domain in serverHold status (payment issue). Needs billing to release hold.
Second occurrence of server hang in under 24 hours. Client requested reboot. Accessed via ILOM KVM console (196.220.49.131) and rebooted. Server came back online.
Network attack incident. Mitigated and monitoring. Active incident.
cPanel/WHM vulnerability remediation in progress. Applying patches across fleet.
Degraded email service since 06/05. Investigation ongoing. Active incident.

Ticket 2700493 follow-up. WordPress block editor POST to wp-json returns 403. Root cause: ModSecurity OWASP CRS rule blocking X-HTTP-Method* headers (likely 921110). Same winsvrweb09 server. Previous fix: 942200 exception for %2C. Needs: add exception for the method-override rule on /wp-json/.* path. Put on hold.

Ticket 2700812. Client could not delete files via cPanel File Manager on lond.co.za (addon domain under otheraccountsco). File ownership mismatch on /dist/ files. Ran chown/chmod via Terminal. Files now deletable. Resolved.

Ticket 2700807. Client Rudolph not receiving emails from notify@nedbank.co.za. Checked firewall/SpamTitan/CSF - no emails found held or blocked. Nedbank sends from 168.142.192.0/24 (mxgw03-18.nedbank.co.za). Likely Nedbank not sending to correct address or issue on Nedbanks side. Advised client to confirm address with Nedbank and forward test email with headers if available.

Ticket 2700738. Customer code 99252. Client Momo reported VPS inaccessible and unable to reach WHM. Clients experiencing password prompts on email accounts. Same incident as previous IMR70-CVPS01 issue - rebooted from VMM yesterday. Server now fully accessible: WHM, cPanel, Exim, Dovecot all responding.

Ticket 2700650. Bjorn Hartnick reported all cPanel websites on srv68 not loading. Coincided with active SRV68 high-priority incident on status page. Server now responding: HTTP/HTTPS, cPanel, WHM all accessible. Resolved.

Ticket 2700650. Bjorn Hartnick reported all cPanel websites on srv68 not loading. Coincided with active SRV68 high-priority incident. Server responding but cPanels still slow. Put on hold pending resolution.

description: Amavisd-new down - Postfix queue stuck with delivery temporarily suspended errors
outcome: Restarted Amavisd-new, flushed queue via postqueue -f. Queue now clear - verified post-flush.
notes: Fully resolved

description: Follow-up: WordPress block editor still returning 403 on POST to wp-json. Root cause: ModSecurity OWASP CRS rule 921110 (HTTP Request Smuggling) blocking X-HTTP-Method-Override: PUT header. Added Plesk WAF exception for rule 921110 on path /wp-json/.*. Client advised on self-service ModSecurity exceptions via KB article and Plesk WAF interface. GET _fields test confirmed working (200).
notes: Client may need to test block editor again. If issue persists, may need to check Plesk audit log for additional rule triggers.

description: Customer Ben Kabuya reports outgoing email not going through. Bounce: 550 5.7.509 Access denied from Microsoft 365 (BW Holdings/Bank Windhoek). Root cause: Customer sends via Mimecast but SPF only authorizes 41.185.64.49 and relay.mailchannels.net. DMARC p=reject causes hard fail on SPF mismatch. Fix: add include:_netblocks.mimecast.com to SPF record. Advised with SPF update and KB links.
notes: Server zwk25-cvps01 (41.185.64.49). Current SPF: v=spf1 ip4:41.185.64.49 include:relay.mailchannels.net ~all

description: CORRECTION: Previous entry incorrectly suggested Mimecast SPF fix. Actual issue: email from lizzelle@ to visagiea@bwholdings.mail.onmicrosoft.com rejected 550 5.7.509. Sending IP 196.46.28.32 (Bank Windhoek) not in SPF. DMARC p=reject causes hard fail. Fix: add 196.46.28.32 to SPF or send via authorized server. Advised customer accordingly.
notes: Server zwk25-cvps01 (41.185.64.49). Customer may be sending from Bank Windhoek network. Current SPF: v=spf1 ip4:41.185.64.49 include:relay.mailchannels.net ~all

notes: dig @ns1.wordpress.com and @ns2.wordpress.com and @ns3.wordpress.com all returned no SOA for CALIBR.CO.ZA. Domain needs to be fully set up on WordPress.com side before registry will accept NS change.

Asked for clarification: which folder(s) empty, any recent mail client changes, POP vs IMAP, and whether webmail also shows missing emails.

description: Investigated disappeared emails report. Confirmed mail files present on disk for all accounts (admin, accounts, careers, director, info, jglenkoe, lmedupe). Dovecot logs show active IMAP logins from client IP (Rain mobile) and ongoing mail delivery (lmtp saving to INBOX at 14:26 and 14:34). Server and mail services functioning normally. Screenshots attached showing emails present on server. Likely client-side issue (POP3 settings, wrong folder, or client config). Advised client to check IMAP vs POP3 settings and verify via Webmail.
notes: PTR: srv56.hostserv.co.za. Remote IP 197.184.109.121 (Rain mobile network) accessed all mailboxes.

description: Emails being pulled from mail storage for director@lenkoeattorneys.co.za. Estimated 1 hour for completion. Client notified.

description: Nameservers updated to thor.ns.1-grid.* and DNS propagated. A record resolves to 41.185.114.18, MX points to 1-grid servers. SPF still needs correction from 41.185.114.10 to 41.185.114.18. Notified client that domain DNS is now active and email can be set up.

description: PHP 8.1 now executing (was serving raw source code due to non-existent LSPHP handler in .htaccess). Rebuilt vhost with --php-fpm. Theme functions.php had unclosed bracket (parse error at line 428) — client reports site now working. Resolved.

malware_pattern.code_in_functions_php: Injected code caused PHP parse error (unclosed bracket) — broke site
malware_pattern.installed_security: Wordfence (installed but did not prevent infection — nulled theme/plugin likely bypassed it)
malware_pattern.symptoms: 500 error, PHP raw source code displayed

description: PHP 8.1 was serving raw source code (non-existent LSPHP handler). Rebuilt vhost with --php-fpm. Then theme functions.php had parse error — moved broken theme, discovered hacklink malware (hacklinkmarket.com / wp_core_check / Telegram backdoor) in ALL theme functions.php files. Cleaned: sed -i to remove malware lines from all themes. Verified clean. Advised: change all passwords, DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT/MODS, update plugins, run Wordfence scan.
notes: Full transcript saved. Malware infected: twentytwenty/functions.php, twentytwentyone/functions.php, twentytwentytwo/functions.php, twentytwentythree/functions.php, twentytwentyfour/functions.php, drenthemev2_broken/functions.php. sed pattern: /hacklink\|hacklinkmarket\|wp_core_check\|Telegram: https:\/\/t.me/d

description: SpamCop report: advertising email for AI Masterclass training sent via srv99 (ashweb.co.za, 41.185.8.171, PTR srv99.hostserv.co.za), advertising chapmanskills.co.za (srv147, 41.185.8.219). DKIM valid, SPF pass, DMARC p=quarantine. Sender origin: rain LTE IP 197.185.169.70 (Outlook desktop). Contact: Karabo Baloyi, admin@ashweb.co.za, Chapman Skills Pty Ltd. Sites both show default cPanel pages. Not a hack — legitimate marketing email reported as spam.

notes: Per session context: Known POPIA violator. Complainant Dan van Eijk never subscribed. Previous 1-grid contact on record — no resolution. Suspension protocol: suspend_outgoing_email + suspendacct per established procedure.

description: WP Site Editor blank screen, REST API pretty permalink broken (/wp-json/ returns 404, ?rest_route=/ works). Add User password button JS broken. Escalated to Sinethemba Kani May 28.
diagnosis: IIS URL Rewrite module not mapping /wp-json/* to index.php. Permalink structure broken on WordPress. Fix: regenerate web.config rewrite rules via Settings → Permalinks → Save in WP admin.

description: Roundcube webmail on srv44 returned "Oops... something went wrong" after login. MariaDB failed to restart with InnoDB corruption error. Root cause: a failed ALTER TABLE in the dekadepa_wpdp database (WordPress Slider Revolution plugin) left an orphan temp file (#sql-alter-44bc-62b3c6.ibd) with duplicate space IDs. Fixed by moving the temp file, starting MySQL with innodb_force_recovery=1, dropping the corrupted wp_dktermmeta table, and restoring it. This is a shared server issue — the corrupted table belongs to a customer WordPress site using Slider Revolution.
outcome: Resolved — MariaDB restarted successfully, webmail operational
resolution: 3. Started MySQL with innodb_force_recovery=1 — MariaDB came up
4. REPAIR TABLE dekadepa_wpdp.wp_dktermmeta failed — "Table doesn't exist in engine"
5. Stopped MySQL, removed recovery config, moved wp_dktermmeta.ibd aside, restarted MySQL
7. Stopped MySQL, moved wp_dktermmeta.ibd back, restarted — MariaDB running cleanly
8. Removed force_recovery.cnf, final restart — webmail working again
symptoms: Webmail (Roundcube) returned "Oops... something went wrong" after login
MariaDB failed to restart: Job for mariadb.service failed
Error log showed duplicate space IDs between #sql-alter temp files and actual .ibd files
Final error: "InnoDB: Plugin initialization aborted with error Data structure corruption"
notes: Key lesson: InnoDB corruption from failed ALTER TABLE. Recovery: 1) Move orphan .ibd 2) innodb_force_recovery=1 3) Drop affected table 4) Restore. The wp_dktermmeta table is part of Slider Revolution plugin - not critical WP core data. The .err log was 744MB.

malware_pattern.type: hacklinkmarket SEO spam injection (same as spectill.co.za)

action_taken: Site suspended due to malware infection. Customer emailed with suspension notice.
suspension_reason: Hacklinkmarket SEO spam injection. Malicious @include in wp-config.php pointing to mu-plugins/index.php. Same infection pattern as spectill.co.za on same server (srv115). Customer notified of suspension.

description: spectill.co.za hacklink malware srv115 injection suspended. Hacklink malware injection on srv115. Malicious file in wp-content/mu-plugins/index.php with @include in wp-config.php. Code injected into all theme functions.php files. Same pattern as epicsolutions.org.za and dren.co.za. Sfundo cleaned files manually. Site restored May 31.

malware_pattern.code_in_functions_php: base64_decode() + eval() obfuscated payload injecting hidden admin user + spam links
malware_pattern.payload_type: Remote payload loader from external server (hacklinkmarket)
malware_pattern.symptoms: 500 Internal Server Error, site down, spam links in site content

remediation: Scan all theme functions.php for base64/eval injection
Remove any unknown admin users from WP database
Update all plugins/themes to latest versions
Run full server-wide scan for cross-infection
suspension_action: Site suspended due to hacklink malware. Client notified.

description: Hacklinkmarket malware outbreak: spectill.co.za (srv115, #2695595, suspended), epicsolutions.org.za (srv115, #2702148, suspended), epicbusiness.co.za (srv115, #2702148, suspended), dren.co.za (srv11, internal, cleaned). All had @include to mu-plugins/index.php + theme functions.php base64/eval injection.

infection_pattern.files_modified: All theme functions.php files injected with base64/eval
infection_pattern.payload: hacklinkmarket SEO spam — hidden admin user + link injection
infection_pattern.code_method: base64_decode() + eval() obfuscated PHP remote payload loader

outcome: All infected sites handled — spectill, epicsolutions, epicbusiness suspended; dren cleaned
remediation_steps: 3. Scan and clean all theme functions.php files
4. Remove unknown admin users from WP database
5. Update all plugins/themes to latest versions
7. Run server-wide scan on srv115 for cross-infection
8. Check for nulled/pirated plugins or themes

findings: DKIM public key in DNS but private key file missing at /var/cpanel/domain_keys/private/isp247.co.za. Exim couldn't sign outgoing emails. MailChannels relay passed unsigned email to Hotmail which rejected with DKIM=Fail.
issue: Hotmail 550 5.7.515 DKIM=Fail - emails from queeny@isp247.co.za rejected
resolution_steps: 1. Checked DKIM selector in /etc/exim.conf (dkim_selector=default) 2. Found DKIM private key missing in /var/cpanel/domain_keys/private/ 3. Generated new private key: openssl genrsa -out /var/cpanel/domain_keys/private/isp247.co.za 2048 4. Extracted public key: openssl rsa -pubout -in /var/cpanel/domain_keys/private/isp247.co.za -out /var/cpanel/domain_keys/public/isp247.co.za 5. Restarted Exim: /scripts/restartsrv_exim 6. Confirmed email was sent via MailChannels (grep in /var/log/exim_mainlog)

resolution_steps: 1. Created database magalies_bpumygxfwq and user on srv144 2. Updated wp-config.php with new DB credentials 3. Disabled Redis (not available on server) 4. Imported SQL database with all tables 5. Granted user privileges 6. Site restored successfully

commands_run: Disabled in WHM → Security → Two-Factor Authentication. Checked access_log for evidence.
findings: WHM-level 2FA enforcement enabled for all cPanel accounts. Access logs showed no user-initiated 2FA changes - likely system/WHM policy push. No evidence of client enabling it.
issue: All cPanels asking for 2FA authenticator not set by user
resolution_steps: Disabled forced 2FA requirement in WHM Security Settings. Advised client to voluntarily set up 2FA on individual accounts.
